evals.name: desktop-ops-direction-smoke
evals.prompt: I am designing a desktop operations app for finance analysts. It needs to feel calm and precise instead of flashy. Give me the design direction, the taste axes, and the main layout rules.
evals.expected_output: Routes through the core principles, picks a fitting style family, sets the taste axes, and adapts the advice for dense desktop work rather than landing-page theatrics.

assertions.text: Response defines the taste axes before discussing decorative details
Agent reads references/platform-adaptation.md for desktop-specific guidance

evals.name: redesign-existing-dashboard-edge
evals.prompt: Our existing dashboard has too many floating cards, weak table hierarchy, and loading states that feel unfinished. I do not want a full redesign from zero. Tell me what to fix first.
evals.expected_output: Uses the critique workflow, prioritizes hierarchy and state fixes before styling, and avoids proposing a ground-up rewrite.

assertions.text: Response prioritizes hierarchy, layout, and state model before polish changes

evals.name: logo-only-negative
evals.prompt: I need a logo mark and mascot illustration for a new coffee brand. Help me craft the visual concept.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the task is outside the scope of this interface-design skill and does not overapply screen-layout guidance.

assertions.text: Does not frame the task as a web, app, or desktop interface problem

evals.name: motion-and-state-disclosure
evals.prompt: The onboarding flow looks polished when static, but hover, focus, loading, and error states still feel cheap. Where should I focus?
evals.expected_output: Routes to the interaction and state guidance, emphasizing state completeness before decorative motion.

assertions.text: Response treats state design as higher priority than extra animation
